observations.recommendation: 1. Management completes the conduct of physical inventory pursuant to COA Circular 2020-006 and submit the RPCPPE.
observations.managementAction: Management requires the reconciliation of records maintained by the Office of the Municipal Accountant with the Property/Equipment Cards maintained by the Office of the Municipal Treasurer and effect the necessary adjustments when appropriate.
observations.reason: This is ongoing and will be completed once the involved personnel are available to devote the necessary time.

observations.recommendation: 4. The Acting Municipal Treasurer updates his cashbooks daily and periodically reconciles his records with the Municipal Accountant in order to ensure that recorded transactions are complete, reliable, accurate and timely.
observations.reason: Reconciling the cashbooks with the accounts is time-consuming, and current time constraints limit the ability to complete the task promptly.

observations.recommendation: 7. Submits a justification and seek DBM’s reconsideration/exemption from the rules as regards the purchased reconditioned motor vehicles. 8. Stops the purchase of secondhand/reconditioned vehicles pursuant to Section 9.1 of the same Circular.
observations.managementAction: Management chose not to submit a letter to the DBM requesting reconsideration, concerned that the DBM would uphold its existing guidelines. Instead, they will defer to the decision of COA on the transaction.

observations.recommendation: 9. Management reviews their organizational structure and staffing to determine the appropriate manpower complement for their programs/ activities/projects. Creation of permanent positions may be considered for regular functions, while hiring of casual or contractual personnel may be considered for projects and activities that are temporary in nature.
observations.managementAction: The positions of Engineer I, Senior Administrative Officer III, and Medical Laboratory Technician III remain under Contract of Service due to the LGU's budgetary constraints.

observations.recommendation: 10. The Municipal Accountant to request write-off of those accounts determined to be uncollectible from the Commission on Audit in accordance with COA Circular 2016-005.
observations.managementAction: According to the Municipal Accountant, there remains an outstanding balance of ₱35,949.93, representing carried-over amounts from 2007 due from various payees. Reconciling and tracing the outstanding balance has proven to be challenging.

observations.recommendation: 11. The Acting Municipal Treasurer establishes the correct turnover of accountabilities between him and the previous Municipal Treasurer.
observations.managementAction: Turnover of accountabilities are yet to be submitted.

observations.recommendation: 12. The Inventory Committee to update the Inventory Count on the identified Obsolete/Unserviceable or Scrap Materials and create an Appraisal Committee to appraise the value said properties. 13. Management facilitates the immediate disposal of these properties pursuant to Section 79 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: Part of the PIP conducted started last Nov. 2023.
observations.reason: The one-time cleansing is still underway.

observations.recommendation: 1.8 We recommended that the Inventory Committee and Municipal Accountant complete the necessary procedures of the one-time cleansing and render appropriate reports to establish the correct balances of the PPE items that shall be reported in the financial statements.

2.6 We recommended that management, in coordination with all Department Heads responsible for supervising and monitoring specific programs, projects, and activities (PPAs), ensure the prompt implementation of any remaining unimplemented PPAs and submit the complete fund utilization reports in compliance with COA Circular No. 94-013.
2.7 Furthermore, for PPAs that have already been implemented but remain unliquidated, we recommended that management expedite the submission of liquidation reports to clear the outstanding balances in the liability accounts and ensure the accuracy and reliability of the financial statements.

3.6. To address the discrepancies between the cash balances in accounting and treasury records, it is recommended that management implement a more structured and frequent reconciliation process between the two departments. It is also important to conduct a thorough internal audit to identify any underlying issues or weaknesses in internal controls, which could be contributing to these discrepancies.

4.4 To ensure timely execution of contracts and avoid delays in the procurement process, it is recommended that the agency consistently adhere to procurement timelines, implement a monitoring system and regularly review processes.

5.7 We recommended and management agreed to immediately comply with established civil service guidelines by filling the vacant regular position through a proper and competitive hiring process, ensuring that qualified candidates are appointed to the Administrative Assistant II (Budgeting Assistant) role. The agency should discontinue the use of contract of service arrangements to fill regular positions to prevent further financial strain and non-compliance with rules.

6.6 It is recommended and management agreed that all activities not otherwise exempt from the coverage of RA 9184, be procured through competitive bidding or any of the alternative modes of procurement sanctioned by law.

7.5. We recommended and management agreed that all future disbursements to the barangays be processed through checks in line with the requirements of COA Circular No. 97-002. This will ensure that funds are properly documented and can be easily tracked, reducing the risk of misappropriation.

8.7. We recommended and management agreed to submit the missing documentary requirements, along with evidence proving that 75% of all planned programs, activities, and projects have been implemented and completed as per the targets for CY 2024, in accordance with Item 5.16 of COA Circular No. 2012-001.

9.5. We recommended that the LGU establish and maintain a verified RPTAR. The Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the Municipal Assessor, should ensure that the register contains accurate, up-to-date information on properties, owners, tax assessments, and payment histories, allowing the Municipal Accountant to properly set up the required Receivable Accounts at the beginning of each fiscal year and ensuring effective tracking of tax revenues.

3. The Municipal Accountant prepares adjusting journal entries to drop from the books the cost of the assets that are not owned and/or controlled by the municipality after due notice of their turn-over in accordance with paragraph 7 of the International Public Sector Accounting Standard (IPSAS) 1 and transfer the same to the books of the recipient barangays. Turn-over documents of these structures have to be attached to the JEV transferring the cost of the structure/s.
5. Management makes the necessary representations with the donor institutions to allow the utilization of the funds for other disaster related programs, projects and activities of the municipality.

6. Management either: a. Identifies projects, programs and activities in the ordinance in sufficient detail so that there would be no need to secure a separate authorization from the Sangguniang Bayan to cover for the specific expense to be incurred or contract to be entered into; or b. Retains the generic line-item in the annual budget but ensure that separate authorization from the Sangguniang Bayan is obtained before entering into any contracts utilizing the allocated funds.

14. We recommend that Management submit to COA an explanation with documentation, if any, on (a) the incurrence of the breach of the contract by the consultant in spite of the presence of the representative of the Planning and Development Coordinator and Municipal Engineer; (b) the loss of ₱126,000.00 paid to the consultant who absconded; and (c) whatever appropriate action the LGU has taken on this case which may serve as the basis of disciplining its officials concerned and/or blacklisting the consultant. If warranted, we may issue a Notice of Suspension/Disallowance to persons/entities found liable of the disbursed amount of ₱126,000.00.

12. We recommended that management review their organizational structure and staffing to determine the appropriate manpower complement for their programs/ activities/projects. Creation of permanent positions may be considered for regular functions, while hiring of casual or contractual personnel may be considered for projects and activities that are temporary in nature.

13. We recommended that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to request write-off of those accounts determined to be uncollectible from the Commission on Audit in accordance with COA Circular 2016-005.
14. We recommended that the Acting Municipal Treasurer establish the correct turnover of accountabilities between him and the previous Municipal Treasurer.

15. We recommended that the Acting Municipal Treasurer regularly update and reconcile his cashbook with the records of the Municipal Accountant at least quarterly to ensure that the recorded transactions are complete, reliable, accurate and timely.

16. We recommended and management agreed to  the Inventory Committee to update the Inventory Count on the identified Obsolete/Unserviceable or Scrap Materials and create an Appraisal Committee to appraise the value said properties.  We also recommend that management facilitate the immediate disposal of these properties pursuant to Section 79 of PD 1445.

17. We recommend that Management submit to COA an explanation with documentation, if any, on (a) the incurrence of the breach of contract by the consultant in spite of the presence of the Planning and Development Coordinator and Municipal Engineer; (b) the loss of ₱126,000.00 paid to the consultant who absconded; and (c) whatever appropriate action the LGU has taken on this case which may serve as the basis of disciplining its officials concerned and/or blacklisting the consultant.  If warranted, we may issue a Notice of Suspension/Disallowance to concerned persons/entities found liable of the disbursed amount of ₱126,000.00.

We recommended and management agreed to:

Complete the conduct of physical inventory pursuant to COA Circular 2020-006 and submit the RPCPPE. 
Henceforth, require the reconciliation of records maintained by the Office of the Municipal Accountant with the Property/Equipment Cards maintained by the Office of the Municipal Treasurer and effect the necessary adjustments when appropriate.

We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting journal entries to drop from the books the cost of the assets that are not owned and/or controlled by the municipality after due notice of their turn-over in accordance with Paragraph 7 of the International Public Sector Accounting Standard (IPSAS) 1 and transfer the same to the books of the recipient barangays. Turn-over documents of these structures have to be attached to the JEV transferring the cost of the structure/s.

We recommended that the Acting Municipal Treasurer update his cashbooks daily and periodically reconcile his records with the Municipal Accountant in order to ensure that recorded transactions are complete, reliable, accurate and timely.

We recommended and management agreed to make the necessary representations with the donor institutions to allow the utilization of the funds for other disaster related programs, projects and activities of the municipality.

We recommended and management agreed to either:

Identify projects, programs and activities in the appropriation ordinance in sufficient detail so that there would be no need to secure a separate authorization from the Sangguniang Bayan to cover for the specific expense to be incurred or contract to be entered into; or

Retain the generic line-item in the annual budget but ensure that separate authorization from the Sangguniang Bayan is obtained before entering into any contracts utilizing the allocated funds.

We recommended and management agreed to:

Submit a justification and seek DBM’s reconsideration / exemption from the rules as regards the purchased reconditioned motor vehicles pursuant to Section 18.0 of DBM Budget Circular No. 2022-1.

Stop the purchase of secondhand/reconditioned vehicles pursuant to Section 9.1 of the same Circular.

We recommended and management agreed to implement the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts pursuant to COA Circular 2020-006 by directing the Inventory Committee to: 

Prepare, in coordination with Property Supply Office and Accounting Office, and submit the PIP for approval to the Head of the Agency to ensure that the conduct of inventory is properly and adequately planned;

Submit a Copy of the approved PIP to the COA Audit Team at least 10 calendar days before the scheduled start of inventory taking activities; and

3. Prepare and submit a consolidated Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant, and Equipment (RCPPE).

4. We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Assessor and Municipal Treasurer reconcile the differences in the RPT and SET assessments.

5. We also recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer furnish the Municipal Accountant with a copy of the duly certified list showing the name of the taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year which should come from the RPTAR/Taxpayer’s Index to be the basis in the recording of RPT and SET Receivables in the books, pursuant to Section 20 of the NGAS Manual, Volume 1.

6. We recommended and management agreed that management increase the fidelity bond of the DO- Designate to such amount sufficient to cover the maximum cash accountability that she handles for a given period in accordance with Treasury Circular No. 02-2009, so as to provide adequate protection for the municipality’s financial resources.

7. We recommended and management agreed that management utilize the LDRRMF only for expenditures which are directly related to disaster prevention and mitigation, preparedness, response, and rehabilitation and recovery in adherence to Section 5.0 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1.

8. We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MDRRMO to strive for the timely and full implementation of LDRRM-related PPAs to achieve the goals and objective of the fund as well as the desired benefits.

9. We recommended and management agreed  to ensure that all disbursements are duly supported with complete documentation prescribed in COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012. It is also recommended that management submit to COA copies of perfected contracts and each of all the documents forming part thereof within five working days from the execution thereof in accordance with Item 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

10. We recommended and management agreed to require the concerned casuals, job orders and contractuals to prepare and submit their individual accomplishment reports duly verified and signed by their immediate supervisors in adherence to COA Circular 2012-001 in order to ensure that services are actually rendered in accordance with their assigned tasks and are truly necessary to meet the service requirements of the municipality.

11. We recommended and management agreed that the different municipal employees concerned perform their respective responsibilities in the proper recognition, monitoring and management of the Local Road Networks in compliance with COA Circular No. 2015-008.

We recommend that management require the inventory committee/team to immediately conduct the physical inventory of all properties and submit the corresponding Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment to the auditor in accordance with Section 124 of Manual on New Government Accounting System (NGAS), Volume1.  Henceforth, require the reconciliation of records maintained by the Office of the Municipal Accountant with the Property/Equipment Card maintained by the Office of the Municipal Treasurer and effect the necessary adjustments when appropriate.

We recommend that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to request write-off of those accounts determined to be uncollectible from the Commission on Audit in accordance with COA Circular 2016-005.

We recommend that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to effect the immediate cancellation/adjustment on the stale checks giving priority to long outstanding prior years issuance in order to reflect the correct balance of the asset account and the  fair presentation of the financial statements at year end.

Also instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the following adjusting entry upon cancellation of the stale check by the Municipal Treasurer:
Cash in Bank- Local Currency Current Account	12,530.00
Payable Account						12,530.00
#

We recommend that management require the Acting Municipal Treasurer to deposit collections intact daily or not later than the next banking day as required under Section 66 of the same manual.  As custodian of government funds and property he has to see to it that no accumulation of collections in his custody occurs to avoid the incurrence of possible loss and misuse of government funds.  Also, demand the restitution of the cash shortage and explanation on the occurrence of the shortage otherwise sanctions will be imposed, if warranted.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant furnish the audit team with the lacking supporting documents. Also, Management is enjoined to be cautious in filling up supporting documents in accordance with standard procedures in the acceptance and inspection of purchased goods/materials in order to avoid the acceptance of goods/services that do not correspond to the specifications in the contract or Purchase Order. Henceforth, all claims against government funds shall be completely supported with the necessary documents pursuant to the provisions of Section 4(6) of PD 1445.

We recommend that management prioritize and consider filling–up the vacant positions to enhance its services and attain full implementation of programs, plans and activities of the local government unit for the optimal benefit of the constituents.

Further, personnel movement has to be effected in order to acknowledge the exemplary performance of employees by way of promotion to higher rank thereby allowing the entry of new applicants for lower level positions.
We recommend the management to fully account the items procured and submit list of beneficiaries with acknowledgement of the items received and explain the deficiencies noted.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant provided the copies of beneficiaries' acknowledgement of rice given attached to the letter of response of AOM last March 2021.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management provide an explanation on releasing the financial assistance to family members of stranded individuals without requiring authorization and proof of receipt from the concerned stranded individuals.  

Henceforth, prepare clear/well-defined guidelines and monitoring strategies to ensure that all programs and projects of the municipality are well implemented and its expected goals are achieved.
observations.managementAction:
A web-based portal was created in which those stranded Corellahanons interested to avail the financial assistance logged all pertinent information asked. The focal person assigned validated the list logged in the portal by contacting the person concerned. 

The LGU submitted copies of the Official Receipt/Sender’s copy last March 2021 (attached to the letter in response to the AOM).

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Health Officer to provide the data of the purchased rapid test kits.  Henceforth, management is enjoined to take note on government accreditations especially on health products to ensure the safety and health of the constituents.
observations.managementAction:
The Rapid Antigen Test Kit that the Municipal Health Office purchased amounting to ₱78,000.00 for Locally Stranded Individuals (LSIs) and Oversees Filipino Workers (OFWs) were of the INNOVITA brand. INNOVITA was included in the initial list of FDA approved Rapid Antigen Test Kits. 

The LGU provided a copy of FDA Advisory No. 2020-483 II FDA Approved Rapid Antibody Test kits for COVID-19 attached to the letter of response of AOM last March 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer and the Municipal Accountant to revisit the items in the Activity Design and for the Municipal Budget Officer to observe proper charging as indicated in the approved activity design.
observations.managementAction: The LGU provided the revised Activity Design and Supplemental Budget for Trust Fund for LDRRMF.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the withholding of taxes to be deducted from any claims for payment and remit the same immediately to the Bureau of Internal Revenue.
observations.managementAction: The LGU applied withholding tax to all succeeding payments to hotel accommodations for returning OFWs.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that in all infrastructure projects implemented by the LGU, that the Municipal Mayor instruct the concerned officials and employees to be mindful on the computation of different deductions pursuant to the provision of the IRR of RA 9184 as this may affect the interest of the local government.
observations.managementAction: The LGU explained that there is no delay noted for the said project, hence the option of limiting the application of 10% retention money up to 50% billing is applicable and not a deviation to the guidelines set forth.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the municipal employee/s assigned to require the appropriate warranty securities for infrastructure projects which are still under the warranty period.
observations.managementAction:
The LGU sent letters to the contractors informing the need to post a warranty security covering form the date of issuance of final acceptance to 1 year as a minimum.

All succeeding projects implemented were already required with warranty security to cover defects after liability period.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to attach the Journal Vouchers to the Disbursement Vouchers together with their supporting documents. These will be the basis for the Municipal Accountant record, classify and summarize all the LGUs transactions.
observations.managementAction: The LGU provided the Audit Team a database of e-NGAS as of December 31, 2020 and moving forward every audit period to have a viewing access of the books of the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Acting Municipal Treasurer establish the correct turnover of accountabilities between him and the previous Municipal Treasurer.
observations.managementAction: The turnover between the previous and current treasurer was properly documented. Records are available in the Municipal Treasurer’s Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Acting Municipal Treasurer regularly update and reconcile his cashbook with the records of the Municipal Accountant at least quarterly to ensure that the recorded transactions are complete, reliable, accurate and timely.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer is slowly updating his cashbooks and the Accounting Office is reconciling the cashbooks with the accounting records.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list showing the names of the taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year as basis of the RPT/SET Receivable in compliance with Section 20 on the MNGAS, Volume I.  We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjustments so that the RPT Receivable and the SET Receivable are of the same amount and also equal to Deferred Real Property Tax Income and Deferred Special Education Tax Income.
observations.managementAction: The LGU is still ascertaining or reconciling the balances.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the different municipal employees concerned perform their respective responsibilities in the proper recognition, monitoring and management of the Local Road Networks in compliance with COA Circular No. 2015-008.
observations.managementAction: On-going inventory of Municipal Road Networks, hence, could not provide an accurate data in the Notes to FS.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the inventory committee exert its best effort to complete the inventory taking and be required to submit inventory reports to the auditor. Further, we advise the Inventory Committee to start the reconciliation of those property items included in the inventory report with the records maintained by the accounting office and property office since their existence and condition have already been established and effect adjustment(s), if any.
observations.managementAction: The LGU was not able to conduct the physical inventory of all properties.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Inventory Committee to update the Inventory Count on the identified Obsolete/Unserviceable or Scrap Materials and create an Appraisal Committee to appraise the value said properties.  We also recommend that management facilitate the immediate disposal of these properties pursuant to Section 79 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: The inventory committee has not yet updated its inventory report regarding the identified obsolete/unserviceable or scrap materials.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management submit to COA an explanation with documentation, if any, on (a) the incurrence of the breach of contract by the consultant in spite of the presence of the Planning and Development Coordinator and Municipal Engineer; (b) the loss of ₱126,000.00 paid to the consultant who absconded; and (c) whatever appropriate action the LGU has taken on this case which may serve as the basis of disciplining its officials concerned and/or blacklisting the consultant.  If warranted, we may issue a Notice of Suspension/Disallowance to concerned persons/entities found liable of the disbursed amount of ₱126,000.00.
observations.managementAction: Management has already submitted three demand letters to the contractor, but still the latter’s whereabouts cannot be determined.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Acting Municipal Treasurer regularly update and reconcile his cashbook with the records of the Municipal Accountant at least quarterly to ensure that the recorded transactions are complete, reliable, accurate and timely.
observations.managementAction: The Acting Municipal Treasurer cannot update his cashbooks regularly due to his workload. The Municipal Accountant inquires if it is acceptable to encode the transactions on an excel file and print the cashbook which would be more efficient than manually writing the transactions in the cashbooks.
observations.reason: During the cash examination, the audit team noted that the cashbooks are still not updated. There is one to two months delay in recording of transactions especially in the general fund due to the number of transactions that occur.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list showing the names of the taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year as basis of the RPT/SET Receivable in compliance with Section 20 on the MNGAS, Volume I.  We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjustments so that the RPT Receivable and the SET Receivable are of the same amount and also equal to Deferred Real Property Tax Income and Deferred Special Education Tax Income.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and Acting Municipal Treasurer based their recording on the Assessor’s records.
observations.reason: For CY 2020, the Real Property Tax Receivable has a balance of ₱557,688.12 while the Special Tax Receivable has a balance of ₱321,514.10. The difference of both accounts refers to previous years’ recording.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the different municipal employees concerned perform their respective responsibilities in the proper recognition, monitoring and management of the Local Road Networks in compliance with COA Circular No. 2015-008.
observations.managementAction: On-going inventory of Municipal Road Networks, hence, could not provide an accurate data in the Notes to FS
observations.reason: The Road Networks System of the municipality is still not disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Local GAD Focal Person through the Municipal Planning and Development Office to gather and keep sex-disaggregated data and to perform gender analysis so as to concretely define its gender issues, to accordingly design programs and activities that could most address them and ultimately to ensure that its fund is utilized to the purpose it is intended.
observations.managementAction: GAD focal person committed to coordinate with the MPDC on this matter.
observations.reason: No sex-disaggregated data and reports on gender analysis mainstreaming was submitted to the Audit Team for verification.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Inventory Committee to update the Inventory Count on the identified Obsolete/Unserviceable or Scrap Materials and create an Appraisal Committee to appraise the value said properties. observations.managementAction: The Acting Municipal Treasurer has been depositing his collections intact and regularly.
observations.reason: During the cash examination on the Acting Municipal Treasurer, it was found that the Acting Municipal Treasurer deposits his collections intact and regularly.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Acting Municipal Treasurer prepare on a daily basis the RCDs consolidating the remitted collections and deposits made, supported with the RCDs of collectors, official receipts and validated deposit slips. If the Municipal Treasurer is fully loaded, he may delegate the preparation of the RCD to his staff.
observations.managementAction: The Acting Municipal Treasurer has delegated his staff in the preparation of the RCDs on a daily basis.
observations.reason: During the cash examination, the audit team noted that the RCDs are prepared on a daily basis.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the liquidation reports, vouchers and MOA of the projects including those projects that have been partially paid and not yet started amounting to ₱22,030,228.00 be submitted for review/audit.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has submitted the liquidation reports and other documents and emailed the data needed with regard to the LGSF.
observations.reason: MOAs have been emailed to the audit team.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant shall observe proper account classification in the recording of releases of financial assistance to implementing agencies and to adhere to the provisions of COA Circular 94-013.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has prepared adjusting journal entries to correct the error and subsequent financial assistance are already booked as receivables pending liquidation.
observations.reason: The audit team has reviewed the correcting journal entries made by the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Acting Municipal Treasurer establish the correct turnover of accountabilities between him and the previous Municipal Treasurer.
observations.managementAction: The Acting Municipal Treasurer, with the help of the Municipal Accountant has established the current turnover of accountabilities.
observations.reason: A copy of the correct turnover report regarding the accountable forms are not yet submitted to the audit team.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that transactions pertaining to the Due to BIR account be traced to prior years’ accounting entries to ascertain the cause of the abnormal balances and effect the necessary adjustments.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant is currently reconciling/tracing transactions of prior years. For the CY 2019 and 2020, the Due to BIR subsidiary accounts are already in order.
observations.reason: Inquiry/ verification is done on the on-going process by the Accounting Department.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Inventory Committee to complete the physical count of inventories and the property, plant and equipment and submit copies of the reports to the Municipal Accountant for reconciliation of accounting records and for the proper recording of adjustments and to the Auditor for verification.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory Committee has conducted the physical count of inventories and the property, plant and equipment.
observations.reason: No inventory report was submitted to the audit team. This has been reiterated in Finding No. 1, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Acting Municipal Treasurer deposit his collections daily or not later than the next banking day to avoid risk of loss or misappropriation of government funds.

We recommend that the Acting Municipal Treasurer prepare on a daily basis the RCDs consolidating the remitted collections and deposits made, supported with the RCDs of collectors, official receipts and validated deposit slips. If the Municipal Treasurer is fully loaded, he may delegate the preparation of the RCD to his staff.

We recommend that the Acting Municipal Treasurer regularly update and reconcile his cashbook with the records of the Municipal Accountant at least quarterly to ensure that the recorded transactions are complete, reliable, accurate and timely.
We recommend that the Acting Municipal Treasurer establish the correct turnover of accountabilities between him and the previous Municipal Treasurer.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list showing the names of the taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year as basis of the RPT/SET Receivable in compliance with Section 20 on the MNGAS, Volume I.  We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjustments so that the RPT Receivable and the SET Receivable are of the same amount and also equal to Deferred Real Property Tax Income and Deferred Special Education Tax Income.

We recommend that management require the Inventory Committee to complete the physical count of inventories and the property, plant and equipment and submit copies of the reports to the Municipal Accountant for reconciliation of accounting records and for the proper recording of adjustments and to the Auditor for verification.

We recommend that the different municipal employees concerned perform their respective responsibilities in the proper recognition, monitoring and management of the Local Road Networks in compliance with COA Circular No. 2015-008.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Local GAD Focal Person through the Municipal Planning and Development Office to gather and keep sex-disaggregated data and to perform gender analysis so as to concretely define its gender issues, to accordingly design programs and activities that could most address them and ultimately to ensure that its fund is utilized to the purpose it is intended.

We commend management for their continuous effort in undertaking these projects and activities for the improvement and development of the municipality for the benefit of its constituents.  We, further, remind the concerned municipal employees to submit the necessary documents for COA technical review and validation/evaluation.
We recommend the agency officials to enforce COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 2997 which governs the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Management properly complied with COA Circular 97-002, hence, no more long outstanding cash advances for cash advances granted in current years.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to adhere to the provisions of Section 48 of the NGAS Manual Volume I in the setting up, payments and liquidation of Petty Cash Fund by implementing the Imprest Fund System.
observations.managementAction: Management is now recording Petty Cash transactions on Imprest System.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management instruct the Municipal Account to effect the necessary adjusting entries in accordance with the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS) and COA Circular 2015-009.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant readily adjusted the accounts upon receipt of the AOM last year.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary journal entries to correct the misclassification and to demand the concerned Punong Barangays and Barangay Treasurers of their immediate submission of liquidation reports in order to properly and timely assess and monitor the project implementation of barangays and fairly present the assets of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant readily adjusted the accounts upon receipt of the AOM last year.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the payroll makers for the reconciliation of the income taxes withheld and their corresponding remittances. Henceforth, compute income taxes in accordance with the TRAIN Law to avoid charges and penalties.
observations.managementAction: The accounting staff has reconciled the differences before her retirement.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Accounting Department use Intra-agency Accounts (Due to/from Other Funds) for transfers from one fund to the other which pertains to withholding taxes in order to properly monitor and reconcile these transactions.
observations.managementAction: Taxes withheld are transferred to the other fund on or before they are remitted as a whole to the BIR.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to trace the accounting entries pertaining to these accounts to ascertain the cause of the abnormal balances and to effect the necessary adjustments.
observations.managementAction: Withholding and remittance are properly reconciled before remittance to the National Government Agency.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Municipal Mayor to designate or appoint a disbursing officer to handle the disbursing functions pursuant to Section 3.1 of COA No. 97-002 and Section 50 of GAAM, Volume III.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Mayor has appointed another disbursing officer.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for the return of the unutilized PDAF of ₱69,840.97 to the National Treasury.
observations.managementAction: Returned the outstanding PDAF balances to the National Treasury.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management follow the guidelines set forth in COA Circular 2017-001 dated June 19, 2017 in requesting for travel claims incurred while on official travel. Subsequent grant of travel claims contrary to the above-mentioned circular shall cause suspension/disallowance of such claim.
observations.managementAction: Claims for taxi fares are properly attached with official receipts.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Vice-Mayor explain the reasons for allowing a casual-plantilla employee to work on a flexi-time basis.  We further recommend that all municipal employees observe the proper office hours as required by the Civil Service Commission.
observations.managementAction: No more casual plantilla on flexi-hour basis.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor designate a permanent employee as Disbursing Officer whose function shall not be transferred to other employees in order to establish proper accountability of the accountable officers.
observations.managementAction: New Disbursing Officer was designated.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the liquidation reports, vouchers and MOA of the projects including those projects that have been partially paid and not yet started amounting to ₱22,030,228.00 be submitted for review/audit.
observations.managementAction: Liquidation Reports due for submission

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant shall observe proper account classification in the recording of releases of financial assistance to implementing agencies and to adhere to the provisions of COA Circular 94-013.
We recommend that transactions pertaining to the Due to BIR account be traced to prior years’ accounting entries to ascertain the cause of the abnormal balances and effect the necessary adjustments.
observations.managementAction: On-going reconciliation/ tracing of negative balances of subsidiary accounts is made by the Accounting Department.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant shall observe proper account classification in the recording of releases of financial assistance to implementing agencies and to adhere to the provisions of COA Circular 94-013.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017, Finding No. 4 | Accounting Department books up financial assistance as donations.
observations.reason: Finding Reiterated

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Inventory Committee to update the Inventory Count on the identified Obsolete/Unserviceable or Scrap Materials and create an Appraisal Committee to appraise the value said properties.  We also recommend that management facilitate the immediate disposal of these properties pursuant to Section 79 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 | Physical inventory always not completed.
observations.reason: Obsolete/ Unserviceable/Scrap materials remain idle.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor designate a permanent employee as Disbursing Officer whose function shall not be transferred to other employees in order to establish proper accountability of the accountable officers.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 | Municipal Treasurer also acts as Disbursing Officer
observations.reason: Finding Reiterated; Manpower shortage

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management create a team that will conduct physical inventory-taking of the Municipality’s properties.
observations.managementAction: 2014 AAR | No Management Action
observations.reason: Management cannot identify Inventory Team

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management submit to COA an explanation with documentation, if any, on (a) the incurrence of the breach of contract by the consultant in spite of the presence of the Planning and Development Coordinator and Municipal Engineer; (b) the loss of P126,000.00 paid to the consultant who absconded; and (c) whatever appropriate action the LGU has taken on this case which may serve as the basis of disciplining its officials concerned and/or blacklisting the consultant.  If warranted, we may issue a Notice of Suspension/Disallowance to concerned persons/entities found liable of the disbursed amount of P126,000.00.
observations.managementAction: CY 2008 AAR | Contractor’s whereabouts cannot be determined
observations.reason: Documents for validation not submitted

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management submit to COA an explanation with documentation, if any, on (a) the incurrence of the breach of contract by the consultant in spite of the presence of the Planning and Development Coordinator and Municipal Engineer; (b) the loss of P126,000.00 paid to the consultant who absconded; and (c) whatever appropriate action the LGU has taken on this case which may serve as the basis of disciplining its officials concerned and/or blacklisting the consultant. observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct all the concerned
employees the complete submission of CY 2017 and every year thereafter year-end
Financial Reports and the related supporting documents at prescribed date of
submission.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer
to deposit all her collections intact daily or not later than the next banking day to
abide by the regulation and to avoid the risk of possible loss of government money.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare and submit the bank
reconciliation statements and trial balances as of December 31, 2017 and every year
thereafter in compliance with COA Circular 96-001 and PD 1445 for the verification
of the Cash in Bank account.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant shall observe proper account
classification in the recording of releases of financial assistance to implementing
agencies and to adhere to the provisions of COA Circular 94-013.

We recommend that management, henceforth, be specific in the terms and
conditions of any agreement/contract entered into, as prescribed by COA Circular
1996-010, to protect the rights of each parties of the contract and to ensure that
government funds are not wasted. Further, we recommend that management
formulate procedures and processes to evaluate the sustainability of the project.

We recommend that management take the necessary actions so that social
pensions will be released to the indigent senior citizens-beneficiaries on the 1st
month of the quarter as prescribed by Memorandum Circular No. 17, Series of
2015. We further recommend that liquidation on the cash advances relating this
transaction be liquidated at once to avoid delay in the release of subsequent funds
from the funding agency.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant 
Designate to maintain complete subsidiary ledgers and prepare the respective
financial statements of the economic enterprises and other major LGU operations to
provide them adequate information in determining the profitability and viability of
operating these economic enterprises.

We recommend that management speed up the implementation of
development projects to attain the desired benefits of socio-economic development
and environmental management outcomes as intended by DILG-DBM Joint
Memorandum Circular 2011-1. Henceforth, the Municipal Planning and
Development Office and the Municipal Engineering Office closely monitor their
implementation to ensure timely completion.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Require the Municipal Accoutnant to submit immediately the financial statements and supporting schedules as of December 31, 2016.
b. Direct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Budget Officer to explain in writing their failure to submit the required financial and budgetary reports.
observations.managementAction: Financial Reports were submitted after the deadline.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management intensify the implementation and monitoring of the above projects in order to deliver good and timely services which the public deserves.
We recommend that the management require the Municipal Accountant to verify the transactions and reconcile the negative balance in order to present a reliable Cash in Bank account of the LDRRM Fund.
observations.managementAction: Erroneous entry adjusted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that issuance of cash tickets to duly designated revenue collectors be properly monitored and controlled as well as their remittances of collections from issuance of cash tickets in accordance with Section 30 of NGAS Manual Volume I.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to update and make the necessary adjustments/corrections in the general/subsidiary ledgers for these to be reconciled with the records of the disbursing officer.
We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer transfer any amount in excess of the allowable maintaining balance to existing bank accounts with authorized government financial institutions (GFIs).

1. Acquire and provide Biometric Machine to LGU personnel to register actual and prescribed office or work hours, as well as to safeguard the signature of the supervisor who will attest the correctness of the DTR of each employee.
2. Require submission of Daily Time Record (DTR) based on the biometric machine entries/records to support in computing the salaries and wages reflected in the payroll transactions of the municipality. In the meantime, carefully monitor and scrutinize log book entries to arrive at the correct man-hours/attendance actually rendered by LGU personnel to avoid overpayment and waste of government funds.

We recommend that management first evaluate whether or not employees are necessary in the respective departments. Department Heads shall first request and explain the necessity to hire job order employees to avoid unnecessary hiring of JOs and waste of government funds. We also recommend that management require the HRMO to establish a system of control in hiring the JO personnel which should basically include the following procedures:
a. Require the applicants to submit PDS, Birth or Marriage Certificates, NBI or Police Clearance and other valid proofs of identity and suitability for the job being applied for;
b. Require applicants to undergo interview and other basic screening process; and
c. Evaluate the performance of JO personnel and determine at the end of the contract period whether there is still necessity of renewing the job orders.

We recommend that the Municipal Budget Officer submit an explanation for allowing the obligation of the LDRRMF for purposes other than those prescribed in COA Circular No. 2012-002 and to utilize LDRRMF only for its intended purposes.
We recommend that management adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 by submitting the Report and Utilization of LDRRMF to COA within the time frame.
observations.managementAction: Reports submitted upon request.
observations.reason: Management overlooked the reporting requirement.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Accounting Office to set up and maintain sub-accounts consisting of separate sets of ledgers for each of the enterprise and consequently prepare separate trial balances and financial statements reflecting the financial condition and results of their respective operations, in accordance with existing regulations.
observations.reason: The enterprise should be self-supporting for the LGU to maintain separate book of enterprise

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly impose the waterworks collection policies in order to maximize its collections. We further recommend that management study other options to encourage the consumers on the prompt payment of their monthly water fees.

We recommend that management trace the transactions pertaining to the Due to BIR account to prior years’ accounting entries to ascertain the cause of the abnormal balances and effect the necessary adjustments.
observations.reason: Implementation adjustments.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to charge and record wages of casual/job order employees assigned in the different offices of the municipality to its appropriate accounts, Salaries and Wages – Casual/Contractual (5-01-01-020) as provided in the Chart of Accounts under the New Government Accounting System.
We recommend that management designate other personnel who does not handle revenue collections as the disbursing officer to avoid incompatibility of functions.

We recommend the Accounting Office to maintain sub-accounts consisting of separate sets of ledgers for each of the enterprise and consequently prepare separate trial balances and financial statements reflecting the financial condition and results of their respective operations, in accordance with existing regulations.
observations.reason: The enterprise should be self-supportin g for the LGU to maintain separate book of enterprise

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to charge and record wages of casual/job order employees assigned in the different offices of the municipality to its appropriate accounts, Salaries and Wages – Casual/Contractual (5-01-01-020) as provided for in the Chart of Accounts under the New Government Accounting System.
observations.reason: Job Order personnel have no employer-emplo yee relationship, therefore, not advisable to charge their wages to the Salaries and Wages account.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management require the immediate refund of the purchase price of the vehicle including the incidental expenses for its repair and maintenance in a total amount of P535,723.00. Otherwise, require the official/staff responsible for effecting the payment to pay the amount of P535,723.00.
observations.managementAction: The transaction has been disallowed.
observations.reason: Supplier’s whereabout cannot be determined.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management submit to COA an explanation with documentation, if any, on (a) the incurrence of the breach of contract by the consultant in spite of the presence of the Planning and Development Coordinator and Municipal Engineer; (b) the loss of P126,000.00 paid to the consultant who absconded; and (c) whatever appropriate action the LGU has taken on this case which may serve as the basis of disciplining its officials concerned and/or blacklisting the consultant. If warranted, we may issue a Notice of Suspension/Disallowa nce to concerned persons/entities found liable of the disbursed amount of P126,000.00.
observations.reason: The whereabouts of the contractor cannot be determined.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Mayor:

   a.  Require the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the  financial
      statements and supporting schedules as of December 31, 2016;


   b. Direct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer  and  the  Budget
      Officer to explain in writing their failure  to  submit  the  required
      financial and budgetary reports.

We  recommend  that  management  intensify  the   implementation   and
monitoring of the above  projects  in  order  to  deliver  good  and  timely
services which the public deserves.

We recommend  that  management  require  the  Inventory  Committee  to
update the Inventory  Count  on  the identified  Obsolete/Unserviceable  or
Scrap Materials and create an Appraisal  Committee  to  appraise  the  value
said  properties.   We  also  recommend  that  management   facilitate   the
immediate disposal of these properties pursuant to Section 79 of PD 1445.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant verify the transactions and
reconcile the negative balance in order to present a reliable Cash  in  Bank
account of the LDRRM Fund.

We recommend that issuance of cash tickets to duly designated  revenue
collectors  be  properly  monitored  and  controlled  as  well   as   their
remittances of collections from issuance of cash tickets in accordance  with
Section 30 of NGAS Manual Volume I.

We  recommend  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  instruct   the   Municipal
Accountant to update and make the necessary adjustments/corrections  in  the
general/subsidiary ledgers for these to be reconciled with  the  records  of
the disbursing officer.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor designate a  permanent  employee
as Disbursing Officer whose function  shall  not  be  transferred  to  other
employees in order to establish proper  accountability  of  the  accountable
officers.

We recommend  that  management  require  the  Municipal  Treasurer  to
transfer any amount in excess of the allowable maintaining balance to  other
existing bank accounts with  authorized  government  financial  institutions
(GFIs).

1. Acquire and provide Biometric Machine to LGU personnel to  register
   actual and prescribed office or work hours, as well as to safeguard
   the signature of the supervisor who will attest the correctness  of
   the DTR  of each employee.

2. Require  submission  of  Daily  Time  Record  (DTR)  based  on  the
   biometric machine  entries/records  to  support  in  computing  the
   salaries and wages reflected in the  payroll  transactions  of  the
   municipality.

3. In the meantime, carefully monitor and scrutinize log book  entries
   to arrive at the correct man-hours/attendance actually rendered  by
   LGU personnel to avoid overpayment and waste of government funds.

We recommend that management first evaluate whether or not  job  order
employees are necessary in the  respective  departments.   Department  Heads
shall first request and explain the necessity to hire  job  order  employees
to avoid unnecessary hiring of JOs and waste of government funds.

We also recommend that management require  the  HRMO  to  establish  a
system of control in hiring the JO personnel which should basically  include
the following procedures:

   a. Require the applicants to submit PDS, Birth or Marriage  Certificates,
      NBI or Police  Clearance  and  other  valid  proofs  of  identity  and
      suitability for the job being applied for;


   b. Require applicants to undergo  interview  and  other  basic  screening
      process; and


   c. Evaluate the performance of JO personnel and determine at the  end  of
      the contract period whether there is still necessity of  renewing  the
      job orders.

We recommend that the Municipal Budget Officer submit  an  explanation
for allowing the obligation of the LDRRMF  for  purposes  other  than  those
prescribed in COA Circular No. 2012-002 and to utilize LDRRMF only  for  its
intended purposes.

We recommend that management create an Appraisal Committee to appraise the assets donated by the Am-Phil Equipment Logistics, LLC in order for the Municipal Accountant to have basis for the cost of the property to be recorded in the books of accounts. Further, we also recommend that disbursements incurred in the transportation of assets to the LGU be capitalized and form part of the cost of donated capital assets.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant make reclassification entries to transfer the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF to a special trust fund for the purpose of supporting disaster risk reduction and management activities within the next five (5) years pursuant to Par.5.1.10 of COA Circular No.2012-002 and Section 3, Rule 18 of the IRR of RA 10121.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to investigate the cause of the abnormal balances and make the necessary adjustments in the books of the agency in order to present fairly the financial statements of the municipality.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the account balances per cashbook and per General Ledger. Adjustments shall be appropriately recorded in the affected books so as to ascertain accuracy of the reported cash balances.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Human Resource Management Officer or the payroll maker to properly monitor and effect the changes of the mandatory deductions and remittance to the national government agencies in order to give the government employees and local government the amount that is due to them.

It is recommended that management stop the practice of granting cash advance for Confidential/Intelligence Fund in excess of one month except on emergency cases but not to exceed the three-month period in order to refrain from incurring excessive expenditures. We further recommend that Municipal Accountant assure the submission and receipt of the liquidations of the cash advances to the COA Central Office in order to provide the certification needed before the cash advances of this kind be granted.

We recommend that management Display notification before the start and while the projects are on-going in accordance with Annex A of COA Circular 2013-004 and to strictly adhere to other provisions of the said Circular.

We recommend management instruct the Municipal Engineer to prepare the Detailed Program of Works/Detailed Plans and Specifications for entire infrastructure project (Evacuation Center) with indication of funding source to facilitate the monitoring of project implementation of the incentive from the Performance Challenge Fund and other fund sources for the project.

We recommend that management strictly impose the waterworks collection policies in order to maximize its collections. We further recommend that management study other options to encourage the consumers on the prompt payment of their monthly water fees.
observations.reason: Management still planning to increase the penalty for late payments.

observations.recommendation: We recommend management create a team that will conduct physical inventory-taking of the Municipality’s properties.
observations.managementAction: Management has started the inventory and PPE count
observations.reason: Time and Manpower Constraints

observations.recommendation: We recommend that transactions pertaining to the Due to BIR account be traced to prior years’ accounting entries to ascertain the cause of the abnormal balances and effect the necessary adjustments.
observations.reason: On-going reconciliation of abnormal balance of the Due to BIR account

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management disburse government funds only upon the approval of proper officials pursuant to Section 4 (5) of PD 1445 and Section 168 of GAAM to avoid disallowance/s.

Require that all Trip Tickets be properly and completely filled up, approved by proper official and signed by all passengers. Enforce the provision of COA Circular No. 77-61 on the preparation and submission of the Fuel Consumption Report and Monthly Report of Official Travels to establish the reasonableness on the consumption of the gasoline, oil and lubricants of every government vehicle. Require the submission of duly accomplished Driver’s Trip Tickets for all usage of government vehicles. Require/cause the marking with "For Official Use Only", the name and logo of the agency to all motor vehicles owned and operated by the municipality.
